contract_summary: batadv_iv_ogm_neigh_new is a get-or-create wrapper for a batadv_neigh_node. It returns a pointer to a neigh_node with an elevated reference count (either by incrementing an existing one or initializing a new one). The caller must release this reference by calling batadv_neigh_node_put when done. Failure (NULL) indicates an allocation failure, so the caller should not use the returned pointer and may optionally retry under different conditions (e.g., after freeing memory).
